stories.title: Trump Is Facing an Increasingly Defiant World
stories.summary: or months and months, President Donald Trump has bullied other countries on everything from trade to how they govern themselves. In just the last few days, however, a handful of global players have defied him, showing the limits of his influence. Iran’s Islamist leaders abandoned peace talks with the U.S., choosing to keep waging war instead. Hungary’s voters tossed out one of Trump’s closest European allies, Prime Minister Viktor Orbán. Then there’s Pope Leo, who presumably answers to a higher

stories.title: Péter Magyar’s real coup was winning over loyal Orbán voters – not preaching to the converted
stories.summary: He is no progressive but unlike the old opposition, the Tisza leader listened to Orbán’s rural base The international audience observing the Hungarian election result is likely to settle on a view that feels familiar. That this election was about east v west or that it was a “youthquake”, a win secured by the unprecedented participation of young voters. These narratives have some truth to them, of course, but, especially for those interested in fighting back against regimes such as Viktor Orbán’

stories.title: Hungary’s incoming PM seeks Polish help to renew EU relations
stories.summary: Péter Magyar hopes building stronger relations with Poland will help restore ties with bloc after Orbán’s rule The Hungarian election winner, Péter Magyar, is eyeing a special relationship with Poland’s prime minister, Donald Tusk to draw on the neighbouring country’s experience of repairing relations with the EU after years of illiberal rule. Since 1989, the two countries have seemingly shared parallels in their paths. Now the two centre-right, pro-European leaders preside over the tricky task

stories.title: Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations
stories.summary: Spirit Airlines reportedly seeks emergency US government funding as war against Iran keeps aviation fuel costs high Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Air Canada has announced a temporary suspension of flights from Toronto and Montreal to New York ’s John F Kennedy airport, citing rising fuel prices. The move comes amid growing concerns that airlines worldwide may scale back services as aviation fuel costs climb in the wake of the US and Israel’s ongoin

stories.title: Orbán's era was over in a flash and Hungary's next PM is a man in a hurry
stories.summary: Péter Magyar and his Tisza party are wasting no time preparing for the transfer of power after their dramatic landslide victory.

stories.title: Kanye West concert canceled in Poland
stories.summary: The Polish government had urged organizers "not to make public space available to promoters of a criminal ideology." Kanye has disavowed and apologized his previous pro-Nazi stance, linking it to his bipolar disorder.

stories.title: Kanye West concert in Poland cancelled over antisemitic comments
stories.summary: The UK government blocked the rapper from entering the country over his history of making pro-Nazi and racist statements.

stories.title: A Tisza 2030-ra felkészítené Magyarországot az eurócsatlakozásra. Mennyire reális ez?
stories.summary: Az euró bevezetéséhez nem elég a politikai döntés, különféle kritériumoknak is meg kell felelnünk hozzá. De ha létrejön egy fenntartható gazdaságpolitika, maga a csatlakozás már nem lesz nehéz.

stories.title: Egy lakásból négy, egy házból kettő: milliós bírságokat hozhat a magyaros ingatlanmatek
stories.summary: A papíron egyetlen lakásként szereplő, de a valóságban több apró apartmanra szabdalt belvárosi ingatlanok és a családi házas övezetbe álmodott, valójában többlakásos hibrid házak korszaka nincs még teljesen leáldozóban, sőt, az igazi problémák csak most kezdődnek. Az önkormányzatok drónokkal és helyszíni szemlékkel vadásznak a rendeltetési egységekkel való trükközésre, a gyanútlan vevőt pedig arra is kötelezhetik, hogy saját költségén falazza vissza a lakását az eredeti állapotra és a korábbi ot

stories.title: Bujdosó Andreát jelöli frakcióvezetőnek Magyar Péter
stories.summary: Bujdosó eddig a párt fővárosi frakcióját vezette.

stories.title: Hosszú órákig álltak sorban az emberek, hogy a Tisza politikusaival aláírassák a plakátjaikat
stories.summary: Leszedték, hazavitték majd tiszás politikusokkal dedikáltatták a szavazók a Tisza választási plakátjait, Nagy Ervin Dunaújvárosban, Tárkányi Zsolt Debrecenben, Szabó Alexandra a Sashalmi piacon írt alá.

stories.title: Győr polgármestere Magyar Péter segítségét kéri a városüzemeltető pénzügyi problémáinak megoldásához
stories.summary: Pintér Bence szerint a Győr-Szol tartaléka 7,5 milliárd forinttal csappant meg az elmúlt években.

stories.title: Bayer: Azoknak nincs mit mondani, akik tapsolnak, ha valaki a fejünkre akarja rakni a heréit
stories.summary: A fideszes véleményvezér a Magyar Nemzetben vonta le a fideszes választási vereség tanulságait.

stories.title: A Tisza nem ismeri el Strompová Viktória vereségét
stories.summary: Magyar Péter felszólította a választókerületben győztes fideszes Ágh Pétert, hogy ne vegye fel a mandátumát.

stories.title: Lengyelországban sem léphet fel Kanye West, a stadion igazgatója lemondta a koncertjét
stories.summary: A Chorzów városában található Stadion Śląski vezetője azt írta, formai és jogi okok miatt nem valósulhat meg a dolog.

stories.title: Először találkozott a parlamentben a két egykori barát, Gulyás Gergely és Magyar Péter
stories.summary: Megtartották a parlamentbe jutott pártok első egyeztetését. Magyar Péter rugalmas, konszenzusra törekvő kétharmadot, és valódi parlamenti vitákat ígér.

stories.title: 140 millió forint támogatást kapott az Orbán Viktorral együtt kampányoló Pásztor Bálint alapítványa a választás előtt egy hónappal
stories.summary: A támogatás a Bethlen Gábor Alapkezelő Zrt.-n keresztül érkezett a magyar államtól.

stories.title: A Tisza Párt teljes Tolna megyét átfordította, Dombóváron is legyőzték a Fidesz jelöltjét
stories.summary: Csibi Krisztina simán nyerte a tavaly januári időközi választást, most azonban kikapott Szijjártó Gábortól.

stories.title: Orbán azt a korrupciót nem tűri, amiben nincs benne – interjú Wáberer Györggyel a Tisza kétharmada után
stories.summary: A választás óta ismét ülésezett a MÁV igazgatósága, ahol a kormányváltás ellenére hosszútávú, több százmilliárd forint értékű szerződéseket akartak áttolni a tagokon. Wáberer György azt is elmondta, milyen nexusa van a Tiszával, illetve hogy miért álljanak ellen azok, akik most dokumentumok megsemmisítését kaphatják feladatnak a minisztériumokban.

stories.title: Wáberer György szerint kizárt, hogy Orbán Viktornak legyen politikai jövője Magyarországon
stories.summary: A Fidesz maffiaszerű kormányzással működtette az országot, és teljesen alkalmatlanok voltak a kormányzásra. Ezt mondta Wáberer György, aki a Tisza Párt győzelme után újra a Telex stúdiójában járt.

stories.title: Könyörögtünk, hogy ne ilyen kampány legyen, írta a Fidesz csepeli jelöltje, aki nemrég még arról posztolt, azon gondolkozik, mi lenne, ha Budapestet bombáznák
stories.summary: Király Nóra szerint semmibe vették azt a kérésüket, hogy legyen más a kampány.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs: A kutatási eredmények alapján mi mindig láttunk egy ösvényt, ami a győzelembe vezet
stories.summary: A Fidesz kampányfőnöke szerint ha dolgok mélyére néznek, láthatták volna, hogy teljesen más stratégia mentén kell menni, de ők győzni akartak, ezért bíztak a saját igazukban.

stories.title: Hormuz standoff shows no signs of letting up for vital shipping to resume from Gulf
stories.summary: Maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz remained at minimal levels on Friday resulting in a giant parking lot, as the US-Iran standoff continues with the same cautious approach since Monday.

stories.title: Sinlaku rips through Northern Mariana Islands as strongest tropical cyclone this year
stories.summary: More than 1,000 people were in shelters across Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands as Sinlaku moved away Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Super Typhoon Sinlaku hammered the Northern Mariana Islands, flipping over cars, toppling utility poles and ripping away tin roofs. Authorities were just beginning to assess the damage left behind by the typhoon, which first hit the islands on Tuesday night local time and continued with a barrage of fierce winds a

stories.title: Humanoid robot chases wild boars in the Polish capital Warsaw
stories.summary: A humanoid robot named Edward Warchocki went viral after chasing wild boars across a parking lot in Warsaw. The Unitree G1 robot has gained attention in recent weeks for its public appearances and interactions. Developed in China, the robot’s personality is a Polish creation.

stories.title: Berlin approves defence initiatives as domestic industry receives drone and frigate deals
stories.summary: New combat drones are planned for use by the so-called Lithuania Brigade, a unit of around 5,000 soldiers who guarantee the defence and security of NATO's eastern flank until the end of 2027.

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: Hungary: Orban election loss reverberates across Central Europe
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

stories.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
stories.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

stories.title: Senate Republicans urge Trump to find Iran exit plan as energy prices rise
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump promised a quick end to the war in Iran, but the ongoing conflict has kept energy costs high — and some Senate Republicans are starting to go public with their concerns. GOP lawmakers who already feared November would be an increasingly tough battle are trying to nudge the president toward clearly defining his endgame after a surge in oil, gas and fertilizer prices. Trump warned the sticker shock might not completely recede by the time the November elections roll arou

stories.title: White House meets with Anthropic CEO amid hopes for a truce
stories.summary: The chief executive of the artificial intelligence startup Anthropic met with top White House officials Friday as anxiety grows across Washington over the cybersecurity risks posed by its powerful new AI model — worries that have thrown a wrench into the Trump administration’s efforts to cut ties with the company. CEO Dario Amodei met in the White House with officials including chief of staff Susie Wiles, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and National Cyber Director Sean Cairncross. Neither side

stories.title: Trump keeps claiming victory in Iran. New POLITICO poll shows voters aren’t buying it.
stories.summary: Seven weeks into the war with Iran, most American voters say President Donald Trump lacks a clear plan and many doubt that Trump is achieving his own goals in the war. New  results  from The POLITICO Poll show that support for military action is weak — just 38 percent of Americans back the strikes — and views  remain largely unchanged  from the days following the joint U.S.-Israel strikes, even as the administration has now had weeks to make its case. A majority of respondent

stories.title: US extends waiver allowing countries to buy Russian oil – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed – follow live coverage on our new blog In case you’re just joining us, here are the latest developments in the Middle East to bring you up to speed. It’s 9am in Beirut and Jerusalem, 9.30am in Tehran and 2am in Washington DC. A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has come into effect , pausing fighting between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 2,100 Lebanese people and displaced more than 2.1 million. The agreement was announced earlier by Donald Trum

stories.title: Young Bulgarians hold out for change in eighth election in five years
stories.summary: Voters broadly split along generational lines as pro-Russian former president leads in polls Anna Bodakova’s days tend to be rather hectic at the moment. Hopping between meeting voters on the street, political debates and recording videos for social media, the 23-year-old is standing to become an MP in Bulgaria’s general election. Last year she was among the many young Bulgarians who participated in countrywide mass protests over the government’s economic policies and perceived failure to tackle

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: tanker reports attack as Iran closes strait of Hormuz; French soldier killed in Lebanon
stories.summary: Iranian military’s command says the strait has ‘reverted to its previous state’; Macron confirms death of French UN peacekeeper Separate to the Pakistani army chief’s trip to Iran (see post at 07:53 ), the Pakistani prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, and foreign minister Ishaq Dar also concluded a trip to the Middle East after visiting Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkey for talks. “We have just concluded the last leg of our engagements following productive and fruitful visits … where we held meaningful

stories.title: Now it’s Republicans blaming American companies for inflation
stories.summary: When Democrats were grappling with sky-high inflation just a couple of years ago, Donald Trump called their plans to fight price gouging by American companies  tantamount to communism . Now that it’s the Republican Party in the affordability hot seat, Trump Cabinet members — and GOP lawmakers on Capitol Hill — are doing much the same, laying into CEOs and calling for new regulations and antitrust suits. “They are making money hand over fist,” Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said of i

stories.title: Canada’s Conservatives ask Carney: Where’s that US trade deal?
stories.summary: TORONTO — Canada’s Conservatives are ratcheting up attacks on Prime Minister Mark Carney for moving too slowly to secure a trade deal with President Donald Trump, opening a new political fight in a dispute with consequences on both sides of the border. This is becoming a wedge issue in Ottawa because the closer the deadline for a review of the North American trade agreement gets, the easier it is for Conservatives to argue Carney is failing to shield Canada from a U.S. trade offensive he has rep

stories.title: Tehran shuts Hormuz again as Iranian gunboats fire on tanker
stories.summary: Iran’s military reimposed strict control over the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, demanding that the U.S. must lift its blockade of Iranian ports before the vital waterway is opened again, a spokesperson for the joint military command said. The move came as Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) gunboats fired on a tanker attempting to transit the strait. “Control over the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state, and this strategic waterway is now under strict management

stories.title: Can Europe avoid a summer of holiday flight and cross-Channel travel chaos?
stories.summary: Passengers face risk of cancellations due to fuel shortages – and long airport queues due to EU entry-exit system What are your rights if your holiday flight is cancelled? Holidaymakers have faced numerous stresses in recent years when planning and budgeting for the sacred summer holiday. Holiday flights to Europe have kept growing despite a pandemic, a cost of living crisis and long airport queues, but summer 2026 threatens to bring fresh anxieties. Legacies of Brexit mean longer border checks

stories.title: Venezuela’s Machado to hold Madrid rally as opposition frozen out after Maduro capture
stories.summary: Exiled leader to revive push for change amid US backing of Delcy Rodríguez and delays to democratic transition Venezuela’s opposition leader, María Corina Machado, will seek to revive her push for political change with a rally in Madrid on Saturday, having found herself sidelined by Donald Trump after the abduction of the president Nicolás Maduro. “Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol squar

stories.title: Mandelson scandal is biggest crisis for diplomatic service in decades, says ex-Foreign Office chief
stories.summary: Simon McDonald says Olly Robbins was ‘thrown under a bus’ by the prime minister and the decision feels wrong • UK politics live – latest updates The Peter Mandelson security vetting scandal is the biggest crisis for the diplomatic service in decades, a former Foreign Office chief has said. Simon McDonald, who was the permanent under-secretary of the government department until 2020, has spoken out in defence of Oliver Robbins, saying the civil servant was “thrown under a bus” by the prime minist

stories.title: As Meloni’s hold over Italy weakens, a progressive challenger gathers momentum in Genoa
stories.summary: Silvia Salis, the leftwing mayor of Genoa and former Olympian, is described as ‘a breath of fresh air’ and potential unifier It has been a turbulent month in Italian politics. A failed referendum on a judicial overhaul pierced prime minister Giorgia Meloni’s aura of invincibility, triggering government resignations and leaving her scrambling to restore credibility. At the same time, her once special relationship with Donald Trump has frayed after the US president publicly scolded her this week f

stories.title: Why are Harvard’s slavery researchers quitting or being fired?
stories.summary: The school’s $100m project to examine its slave ownership in Antigua is mired with controversy as academics allege obstruction Christopher Newman remembers seeing campus police officers as he walked into a human resources office at Harvard University , but he didn’t imagine that they were there for him. It was July 2024, and Newman had just turned in the results of a two-month-long internship with the Harvard University Archives: an annotated bibliography for the landmark 2022 Harvard and the Le

stories.title: Norwegian politicians hope Epstein files inquiry will restore faith in democracy
stories.summary: Disgraced financier’s links to politicians and civil servants as far back as 30 years ago to be examined The Epstein files have shaken Norway’s faith in democracy, the head of the Norwegian parliament’s oversight committee has said, as a sprawling investigation into the connections between its foreign office and the late sex offender gets under way. An independent commission to look into information brought to light by the Jeffrey Epstein documents released by the US Department of Justice was la

stories.title: Trump’s antipathy for Pope may have roots in childhood Protestant church
stories.summary: Manhattan church led by Norman Vincent Peale was known for opposing presidency of JFK – and Catholics in general Donald Trump ’s attacks this week on Pope Leo , for his criticism of the US attack on Iran and the US president’s decision to post an image of himself as Jesus Christ on social media, make a good deal more sense considering Trump attended services as a young man at the Protestant Marble Collegiate church in Manhattan, which was led at the time by an anti-Catholic pastor. That church’s

stories.title: NSW should think twice before banning ‘globalise the intifada’ after court struck down anti-protest law, legal expert says
stories.summary: Another lawyer says ruling ‘puts brakes on the Minns government’s ability to use executive power to minimise people’s rights to protest’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Minns government should think twice before imposing an outright ban on the phrase “globalise the intifada” in the wake of a landmark finding that could limit attempts to control speech and protests, a leading constitutional expert has said. New South Wales’ highest court ruled in favour of the Pal

stories.title: Germany news: Iran war not causing migration wave — Dobrindt
stories.summary: German Interior Minister Dobrindt says that no increased migration to Europe has so far occurred amid the Iran war. An explosion in an underpass in a western town has killed one person. DW has more.

stories.title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
stories.summary: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

stories.title: BBC reports from Lebanese border town as residents try to return home
stories.summary: BBC Arabic's correspondent Carine Torbey reports from the border town of Khiam, as the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon goes into its second day.

stories.title: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.title: ‘We are not going back’: Iran war forces global energy shift
stories.summary: This week’s gathering of financial heavyweights in Washington made one thing clear: The Iran war is setting the world on a new energy path. But which one is a source of sharp disagreement among nations. Many of the countries that are most severely affected by the war say they recognize the benefits of transitioning faster to renewable energy to avoid future shocks related to oil disruptions. Others remain bullish on fossil fuels, including the United States — whose Treasury secretary, Scott Bess

stories.title: Iran threatens to close Hormuz again if US blockade continues
stories.summary: The speaker of Iran’s parliament issued a direct threat to close the Strait of Hormuz again if a U.S. naval blockade continues, marking one of Tehran’s sharpest public warnings since ceasefire negotiations began. “With the continuation of the blockade, the Strait of Hormuz will not remain open,” Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf wrote on X just after midnight Friday, in a series of posts that also accused U.S. President Donald Trump of making seven false claims “in one hour.&

stories.title: I want to reform our country because a strong Germany is a precondition for a strong Europe | Lars Klingbeil
stories.summary: The war in Iran has exposed our dependencies. Europe, including the UK, must be bold about change, so nobody can blackmail us Lars Klingbeil is Germany’s finance minister and vice-chancellor Wars and crises are draining our economies, our sense of security and our emotional wellbeing. They are affecting our daily lives: supply chains are becoming less reliable, energy prices are soaring, and trade dependencies on fossil-fuel energy and critical minerals pose risks to national security. Tariffs,

stories.title: Six great reads: Iran’s social media memes, an abandoned department store and a 1,200-year-old record of cherry blossoms
stories.summary: Need something brilliant to read this weekend? Here are six of our favourite pieces from the last seven days Continue reading...

stories.title: Stranded and dying, the German whale is a parable of our troubled relationship with these sea giants
stories.summary: Even as we empathise with these intelligent animals, our relentless push for resources kills them in their thousands, just as whalers once hunted them to the brink of extinction For weeks now, a humpback whale has been trying to die. Entangled in ropes, it had wandered into the shallow Baltic Sea . Unable to feed, it is now subject to extreme dehydration, since whales satisfy their thirst through the fish they eat. In such a parlous situation, the whale’s last resort was to strand itself on Poel

stories.title: Father of man who inspired Super Mario was also named Luigi, researcher finds
stories.summary: Elisabeth Zetland, a senior researcher at MyHeritage, found that the actual Luigi had immigrated to US from Italy Gaming enthusiasts have known for years that Nintendo named its mustachioed, superhero plumber after the company’s landlord, Washington state businessman Mario Arnold Segale. But it has only just been determined that Nintendo may have unknowingly named Super Mario’s fictional brother after Segale’s real-life father: Luigi, whose biography evokes that of millions of 20th-century US im

stories.title: What are my rights if flights are cancelled and holidays disrupted due to fuel shortage?
stories.summary: Iran war and EU’s new entry-exit system could lead to travel misery this summer, but there are ways to mitigate it Can Europe avoid a summer of travel chaos? The war in the Middle East has prompted fears that potential shortages of jet fuel could result in flight cancellations this summer and warnings that holidays could be severely disrupted. Airports have said jet fuel could run short within three weeks as a result of supply problems after the strait of Hormuz was effectively closed when war b

stories.title: ‘It’s kind of a tough situation’: US Catholics torn in feud between president and the pope
stories.summary: Trump’s attacks on Pope Leo XIV polarizing the diverse community as faith and politics come to a head Maryellen Lewicki meets once a week for Bible study with a group of Catholic women in Decatur, Georgia, in a space they try to keep clear of politics. But Donald Trump’s name arises nonetheless. “We have one person that we pray for during the course of the week,” she said. “What my friend said is that she prays for the president every day, that God will remove that hard heart of his and replace

stories.title: Earth gets brighter every year but progression is volatile, study finds
stories.summary: Covid, light pollution regulations and faltering global economy affect location and intensity of brightness Earth continues to get brighter every year, researchers have found, but the location and intensity of the progression has become increasingly volatile because of Covid-19, regulations on light pollution, and a faltering global economy. Nasa-funded researchers at the University of Connecticut (UConn) studied more than 1.1m satellite images taken over a nine-year period to establish that the

stories.title: Survivors of alleged sexual abuse by former owner of Harrods want enablers to face justice
stories.summary: Justice for Fayed and Harrods Survivors group claim there are ‘dozens of individuals who must be held to account’ A group of 50 survivors of alleged sexual abuse by Harrods’ former owner Mohamed Al Fayed are calling for “meaningful consequences” for those who they claim facilitated and ignored the abuse. “If they think the money is the important factor they are so far off the mark,” said Jen Mills, a member of the Justice for Fayed and Harrods Survivors group. They claim there are “dozens of ind

stories.title: Counter-terror police investigate arson attack in north-west London
stories.summary: Met describe ‘similarities’ with other recent attacks after business in Hendon was targeted on Friday Counter-terrorism police are leading an investigation into an arson attack on a business in Hendon, north-west London. The force said that, while it was not yet being linked to arson attacks on a nearby synagogue and Jewish ambulance charity, counter-terrorism officers were being deployed owing to “similarities” between the incidents. Continue reading...

stories.title: Fourth suspect in London arson attack on Jewish ambulances remanded in custody
stories.summary: Judex Atshatshi, 18, appears in court over what prosecutors say they believe was targeted attack on Jewish community A fourth suspect charged after four Jewish community ambulances were torched in north-west London has been remanded in custody. Judex Atshatshi, 18, a British national from Dagenham, east London, appeared at Westminster magistrates court on Saturday, charged with arson with intent to damage property and being reckless as to whether life would be endangered. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Whatever life throws at us, we don’t walk alone’: how a London synagogue attack birthed an act of solidarity
stories.summary: After the attempted arson attack on a London synagogue, communities remain determined that ‘building higher walls’ will not stem rising tide of antisemitism “How good and how wonderful it is when friends sit together,” reads a quote from the Psalms painted high on the wall inside Finchley Reform Synagogue (FRS). For the congregation gathering in a cheerful hubbub before its Shabbat service on Friday evening, that felt like an especially apt sentiment. Three days after the synagogue was the victi

stories.title: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’
stories.summary: IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait after brief window when strait had reopened • Middle East crisis – live updates Iranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports. A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Sat

stories.title: Olly Robbins ‘thrown under bus’ by No 10, says ex-Foreign Office chief – UK politics live
stories.summary: Sir Simon McDonald said No 10 ‘wanted a scalp’ but removal of Robbins from Foreign Office felt ‘wrong’ Keir Starmer faces ‘judgment day’ Five unanswered questions On Friday the Cabinet Office released a template page from the summary document produced by UKSV after Mandelson’s vetting. The document would be used by a vetting officer to summarise their findings. It lists three rankings for possible “overall concern”: low, medium and high. In the next box, there is a space for a vetting officer to

stories.title: ‘We can’t wait’: Venice already seeking floods plan B five years after barriers’ launch
stories.summary: Rising sea levels and ecological damage caused by heavy use of flood defence system force city authorities to consider next move The Arsenale, the colossal shipyard that was the engine of the Venetian Republic’s domination for seven centuries, remains the nucleus of the city’s control over the water. Its northern section is made up of cavernous brick warehouses called capannoni , which in the 16th century could produce a warship a day through a rigorously ordered assembly line. Now, one of them

stories.title: Jacinta Allan wants voters to see Victoria’s 12-year-old Labor government as ‘new and united’. Can she cling to power?
stories.summary: With a reshuffled cabinet, the premier is hoping to quell leadership rumblings as her party seeks an unprecedented fourth term Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast As the Victorian premier, Jacinta Allan, stood alongside the fresh faces in her reshuffled cabinet on Wednesday, she attempted to send her increasingly jaded electorate a blunt message: despite its 12 years in power, her government is – apparently – new. In her opening four-minute preamble to reporters, Allan -

stories.title: US extends waiver allowing purchase of Russian oil
stories.summary: The US has renewed a waiver permitting countries to buy Russian oil for another month. The decision came two days after the Trump administration had said it had no plans to extend the waiver.

stories.title: India: Parliament fails to pass women's quota bill after delimitation uproar
stories.summary: Indian government's bid to redraw voting boundaries tied to quotas for women legislators was blocked in a parliament vote. It is the first major legislative setback for PM Modi's government. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Displaced by Iran war: out of Lebanon, into Syrian crisis
stories.summary: More than 227,000 people have fled conflict in Lebanon for Syria. But there they have found a lack of housing, economic hardship and no real state services. Despite a new ceasefire, many say they will stay in Syria.

stories.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
stories.summary: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

stories.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again over US blockade
stories.summary: Iran has accused the US of violating the ceasefire agreement by maintaining a blockade on its ports. Meanwhile, a French peacekeeper was killed in southern Lebanon. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Iran: Internet blackout highlights real toll of censorship
stories.summary: For Iran's government, the internet shutdown is about control. But for millions of ordinary Iranians, it is a daily reminder that access to the wider world, and even running a business, is controlled by the state.

stories.title: Iran says it is to resume 'strict management' of Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran announced on Saturday it would resume "strict management" of the Strait of Hormuz, reversing a decision to unblock the strategic channel as part of negotiations with the US. They said Washington had broken a promise by continuing its naval blockade of ships sailing to and from Iran's ports.

stories.title: Lebanon president says his country is in 'a new phase' after Israel truce
stories.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun declared Friday that the newly brokered ceasefire marks the start of a "new phase" for Lebanon, vowing the country will move toward permanent agreements with Israel.

stories.title: Starmer faces calls to step down after appointing Epstein associate
stories.summary: Opposition leaders demanded Starmer's resignation on Thursday after it emerged that US envoy Peter Mandelson was appointed despite having failed his security check.

stories.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
stories.summary: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

stories.title: Rome and Caucasus poised to further discuss closer energy ties
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister is expected to visit Azerbaijan in the coming weeks to continue political dialogue and energy cooperation between Rome and Baku, with Azerbaijani gas supplies via the Trans Adriatic Pipeline remaining a key pillar of bilateral ties.

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith’s comrades say he ordered them to execute unarmed civilians, court documents show
stories.summary: Former SAS corporal allegedly placed man on his knees and ordered fellow soldier to shoot him, according to statement of facts Australian soldiers have told prosecutors they executed unarmed civilians at the orders of Ben Roberts-Smith or in complicity with him, according to a statement of facts tendered to the New South Wales local court. Roberts-Smith, a Victoria Cross recipient and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of the war crime of murder, allegedly committ

stories.title: Sanctions don’t result in regime change. Whether against Iran or Russia, western countries need shrewder tactics | Simon Jenkins
stories.summary: Dissent cannot take hold in soil rendered barren by emigration and embargo. Political opposition needs academic and cultural exchange if it is to prosper The chancellor of the exchequer and the IMF agree . Britain’s economy is about to take its greatest hit for decades. This is collateral damage from the US’s war on Iran and the closing of the strait of Hormuz – and will be made worse by sanctioning Gulf oil exports . Britain has already been weakened by four years of sanctions against Russia ov

stories.title: Lebanon truce takes effect as damaged Qasmiyeh bridge reopens
stories.summary: Lebanon and Israel entered a 10-day truce early Friday, as the army reopened a single lane on the damaged Qasmiyeh Bridge in the south after Israeli strikes hit the key crossing linking Tyre to the wider region.

stories.title: Kenyan firm sacks more than 1,000 workers after losing Meta contract
stories.summary: Meta paused work with Sama last month after allegations about staff viewing private scenes filmed by smart glasses More than 1,000 low-paid workers in Kenya have been abruptly sacked by an outsourcing company contracted by Meta, in what activists said was a shocking move exposing the precariousness of tech jobs in the global south. Sama, a company based in Nairobi to which Meta outsourced content moderation and AI training work, announced on Thursday that the workers were being laid off after Me

stories.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
stories.summary: Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map. It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament. Continue reading...

stories.title: Inside smoky shelters, a fast-paced, illegal card game has taken off in Solomon Islands
stories.summary: Growing numbers in the capital Honiara are playing the street card game Pass for a chance of a big payout, while risking big losses As the school day ends in Honiara, *Irene, a 43-year-old teacher in a floral dress with a yellow daisy in her bun, steps on to a minibus. After 10 minutes, Irene gets off the bus, walks down an alley, and enters a damp, smoky shelter. Plastic tables fill the space and playing cards are scattered on the floor. Irene has stopped by a hidden gambling table in a western

stories.title: Iran war: Strait of Hormuz open amid Israel-Lebanon truce
stories.summary: As a 10-day ceasefire deal between Lebanon and Israel took effect, the Israeli military said its forces are going to remain in southern Lebanon. Iran and Donald Trump say the Strait of Hormuz is now open.

stories.title: India’s harvest festivals under climate strain
stories.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

stories.title: Iran war: What does Tehran expect from fresh talks with US?
stories.summary: Amid talk of a second round of negotiations between Iran and the US, experts say a breakthrough between the two sides is possible, but far from certain.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV to Cameroon's faithful — 'reject violence'
stories.summary: Some 120,000 cheering Catholics attended Pope Leo XIV's Friday mass in Cameroon. The pontiff implored those gathered to be the architects of peace and to reject the deceit of violence and abuse.

stories.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
stories.summary: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.

stories.title: India: Modi plan for women's quota in parliament fails vote
stories.summary: Opposition parties accused the government of using the quota to increase its own influence in the Indian parliament. The proposal would have seen hundreds more seats added and a 33% quota for women.

stories.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
stories.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

stories.title: Can the 25th Amendment remove Donald Trump from office?
stories.summary: After Donald Trump threatened to wipe out "an entire civilization," clashed with the pope and posted an AI image of himself as Jesus, US Democrats and some Republicans are questioning his fitness to serve as president.

stories.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

stories.title: Global sigh of relief and mixed messages as US and Iran say Strait of Hormuz open
stories.summary: Iran and US declare Strait of Hormuz "completely open," but Iran adds that passage is on a "coordinated route" set by Tehran.

stories.title: Three charged with arson on Persian media offices in London
stories.summary: Two teenagers and a 21-year-old man appear in court charged with arson with intent to endanger life.

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.summary: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Venezuela and EU set out roadmap for renewed ties amid political thaw
stories.summary: Following talks in the Venezuelan parliament, Caracas and the EU have agreed on a new cooperation agenda. The shift aligns with a broader economic pivot, as the nation resumes ties with the IMF and the World Bank.

stories.title: 'I understand when you say no to war,' Brazil's Lula tells Spain's PM Pedro Sánchez
stories.summary: Lula and Sánchez are both outspoken in their criticism of US President Donald Trump, who has threatened both with punitive tariffs.

stories.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
stories.summary: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire: civilians return to ruined towns in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A ceasefire in Lebanon has brought a pause in fighting after weeks of war. Residents are returning to damaged homes as questions remain over how long the truce will hold.

stories.title: White House vs the pope: What is behind the clash and Catholic just war doctrine?
stories.summary: Theologians Euronews spoke to believe that the escalating war of words between Washington and the Holy See has raised important questions over Catholic and Christian moral thought.

stories.title: What next for South African opposition firebrand Malema after his five-year prison sentence?
stories.summary: The sentence raises huge questions marks over the political future of one of South Africa's most controversial politicians.

stories.title: 'Mum is finally free!' Pensioner detained by ICE in US returns to France
stories.summary: Marie-Thérèse had moved to the US after reuniting with her long-lost love, an American man who died in January.

stories.title: Suspected crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
stories.summary: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

stories.title: First cruise ship transits the Strait of Hormuz since Iran war began
stories.summary: Iran and the US said the strategic waterway, which has been effectively closed since the beginning of the war, will be fully open to commercial traffic for the remainder of the ceasefire

stories.title: Japan reveals new name for 40C-and-hotter days after blistering summer
stories.summary: The term - kokushobi - translates to "cruelly hot", "brutally hot" or "severely hot", and comes after Japan's hottest summer on record.

stories.title: Thousands celebrate open-air Mass with Pope Leo in Cameroon - in pictures
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV is on his third day in Cameroon before he heads to Angola on Saturday.

stories.title: Officer on horseback chases suspect through New York streets
stories.summary: The suspect was accused of purse-snatching and has been charged with larceny and providing false information.

stories.title: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
stories.summary: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

stories.title: What does it take to survive in the Arctic? These rangers have an idea
stories.summary: The BBC spent time with the Canadian military in the country's northernmost region - this is what they taught us about dealing with extreme conditions.

stories.title: Three sentenced for 'man in bear suit' insurance scam
stories.summary: Video went viral after investigators determined the purported ursine was actually a man in a costume.

stories.title: Watch: Fuel truck leaves trail of fire on road in Syria
stories.summary: Eyewitnesses captured the moment a burning fuel tanker drove through the centre of Hasakah, Syria, leaving a trail of fire behind it.

stories.title: White House and Anthropic hold 'productive' meeting amid fears over Mythos model
stories.summary: The discussion is a sign the AI firm's technology may be too critical for even the US government to do without.

stories.title: Az iráni háború felgyorsítja a zöldenergia felfutását, és ezzel pénzt is lehet keresni
stories.summary: A háború előtérbe hozta az energiabiztonsági kérdéseket, miközben sokat javult a megújulóenergia-források versenyképessége. Ez tükröződik is a zöldenergia-részvények kiemelkedő teljesítményében.

stories.title: Akkora az orvoshiány, hogy még a 87 évesen praktizáló orvosra is számítanak
stories.summary: Tömeges az orvoshiány Bulgáriában, a fiatalok külföldre mennek a magasabb fizetésekért

stories.title: A NASA hipnotizált egy asztronautát, hogy ne káromkodjon a Holdon
stories.summary: Az űrügynökségnek meggyűlt a baja az élő közvetítésben szentségelő asztronautákkal, unortodox megoldást választottak a probléma kezelésére.

stories.title: Segíts nekünk kiteregetni a NER gazdasági hátországának titkait!
stories.summary: Történelmi pillanat és lehetőség a mostani, de olvasóinkra is szükségünk van, hogy még többet kiderítsünk a lebomló rendszer piszkos ügyeiről. Mielőtt még eltűnnek a nyomok.

stories.title: Trump szerint egy csodás napra ébredtünk, Hszi Csin-ping is örül a szoros újranyitásának
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök szerint ha nem sikerül szerdáig megegyezni, véget érhet a tűzszünet.

stories.title: Ők már biztosan ott lesznek a G7 Paradigmán
stories.summary: A MOL, Richter, AutoWallis, HOLD, Oriens, Siemens Energy, Bosch, Egyensúly Intézet, Videoton Holding és Kopint-Tárki vezetői tekintik át a helyzetet áprilisi konferenciánkon.

stories.title: Önként rendőröknek átadott kábítószer, elviselhetetlen propaganda és szétcringelt Jaber – erős részletek a ByeAlex-interjúból
stories.summary: A zenész részletesen beszélt a nála tartott házkutatásról és arról, hogy Jaber kampányba dobása már olyan volt, mint egy rossz South Park epizód.

stories.title: ByeAlex: Biztos voltam benne, hogy a Fidesznek ez gatya
stories.summary: Az előadó szerint a Fidesz mellé állított celebek öngólok voltak, és leginkább Jaber kiállása ment mélyre nála. ByeAlex a nála tartott házkutatásról is részletesen beszélt, és azt is elmondta, miért nem vállalta a rendszerbontó koncertet.

stories.title: Hadházy: A Mészároshoz köthető vadásztársaság már kilenc éve tart zebrákat, köztük Pizsit, Szuszit és Tihamért
stories.summary: A képviselő két hónap után kapott választ a Nébih-től.

stories.title: Fegyverrel fenyegetőzött egy közveszélyes sofőr a Hungária körúton
stories.summary: Majdnem elütött egy biciklist, többször büntetőfékezett, majd azzal fenyegetőzött, hogy szájba lövi az egyik autóst.

stories.title: A péntek volt az év eddigi legmelegebb napja
stories.summary: Dabason 25 fok volt, igaz, a Kékesen csak 14,8.

stories.title: Nagy Feró: Vasárnap győzött a demokrácia
stories.summary: A Kossuth-díjas művész szerint gyűlöletszavazás történt.

stories.title: Azért költötték el a 686 millió forintot a kutyapártosok, mert fel sem merült bennük, hogy nem érik el az egy százalékot
stories.summary: A Kutyapárt gyűjtést indított az állami támogatás visszafizetésére, mert nincs ennyi pénzük. Váratlanul érte őket a választási eredmény, és azért költötték el a teljes támogatást, mert úgy gondolták, jobb helyen van náluk a pénz, mint az államnál.

stories.title: Egyre több választókerületben van meg a 100 százalékos feldolgozottság, kevés helyen várható már fordulat
stories.summary: A külképviseleteken leadott és az átjelentkezőktől beérkező szavazatokat számolják még ma, aztán meglehet a nem hivatalos végeredmény.

stories.title: 21 Kutatóközpont: Többségbe kerültek azok, akik abban bíztak, hogy lehet jobb, mint azok, akik attól féltek, hogy lehet rosszabb
stories.summary: A választás véghajrájában közvélemény-kutatást készített a 21 Kutatóközpont, a válaszadókat arról kérdezték, kire szavaztak korábban, miért akarnak vagy nem akarnak kormányváltást, és milyen várakozásaik vannak.

stories.title: Irán újra szigorú katonai felügyelet alá vonta a Hormuzi-szorost
stories.summary: A katonaság szerint ez így is marad, amíg az USA nem oldja fel a blokádját.

stories.title: 2032-ig meghosszabbították Trócsányi László rektori megbizatását a Károlin
stories.summary: Az egykori nagykövet és EP-képviselő 2022 óta vezeti az egyetemet.

stories.title: „Vlagyimir Vlagyimirovics, az emberek félnek tőled” – felszólalt az elnök ellen egy népszerű orosz influenszer
stories.summary: Veronica Bonya kezdi azt érezni, hogy már nem egy szabad országban él, de azért támogatja Putyint. Sok orosz influenszer is hasonló témában posztol.

stories.title: A tiszás Strompová Viktória nem tudott fordítani, a fideszes Ágh Péter nyerte a sárvári választókerületet
stories.summary: A külképviseletekről és az átjelentkezőktől beérkezett szavazatok beszámítása után 258 szavazat volt különbség a két jelölt között.

stories.title: Több ezer civil és aktivista bénította meg a Fidesz szavazatszerző gépezetét
stories.summary: A szavazat ára című film mindig is egy eszköz volt arra, hogy a választás napján lelkes önkéntesekkel megakadályozhassák a szavazatvásárlást. De pontosan mi történt a választás napján a gyanús körzetekben? A 2026 legújabb adásában Tímár Áron, A szavazat ára ötletgazdája és Bódi Mátyás, a Választási Földrajz szakértője.

stories.title: Még egy mandátum összejöhet a töredékszavazatokból a Tiszának
stories.summary: Akár 141 mandátuma is lehet a parlamentben a Tiszának.

stories.title: Robbanás történt éjszaka egy németországi aluljáróban, egy ember meghalt
stories.summary: A rendőrség még nem tudja, mi robbanhatott fel.

stories.title: Az ország legfideszesebb választókerületében is megvan a végleges eredmény, nyert Gyopáros Alpár
stories.summary: A Fidesz-KDNP jelöltje 1,16 százalékkal szerzett több szavazatot a Tisza jelöltjénél Győr-Moson-Sopron 3-as számú egyéni választókerületében.

stories.title: Összejött az extra mandátum a töredékszavazatokból a Tiszának
stories.summary: Így 45 listás mandátumot húztak be.

stories.title: Egész sokat megtudtunk már a Tisza gazdasági terveiről
stories.summary: Az új kormány csak lassan, esetleg fokozatosan nyúlna hozzá az olyan piactorzító elemekhez, mint az árrésstop vagy az üzemanyagok védett ára, hogy a kivezetésük minél fájdalommentesebb legyen.

stories.title: Nagy amatörizmusra vallana, ha most kezdenének iratokat darálni a minisztériumokban
stories.summary: Ritkán semmisítenek meg végleg köziratokat, de ettől még az ilyesmi a mindennapi működés része a minisztériumokban. Ma már egyáltalán nem olyan egyszerű kompromittáló dokumentumokat eltüntetni, mint akár csak 20-30 éve.

stories.title: Hahó, vége a választásnak, ideje lenyugodni! Irány a Szekszárdi-dombság!
stories.summary: A választási izgalmak után sokaknak szükségük lehet egy kis nyugodtságra, higgadásra. Az egyik legjobban fekvő túraterep ehhez a Szekszárdi-dombság szelíden hullámzó, békés tája.

stories.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
stories.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

stories.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
stories.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

stories.title: ‘Offline Day’: Emmanuel Macron promotes books and theatre as alternatives to screens
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron met with pupils in Villers-Cotterêts to promote a new “Offline Day” initiative aimed at reducing screen time among young people. The visit took place at the Cité internationale de la langue française, where students participated in reading and theatre workshops.

stories.title: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
stories.summary: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

stories.title: Ankara pushes deeper economic ties with Astana ahead of Erdoğan visit
stories.summary: Astana and Ankara aim to boost trade, diversify exports and launch joint industrial projects ahead of Erdoğan’s state visit to Kazakhstan, setting a goal of $15 billion in trade volumes.

stories.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
stories.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

stories.title: Military operation against Hezbollah 'still not complete,' Israel's defence minister says
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a 10-day ceasefire on Friday after a meeting between the Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors, the first direct diplomatic talks between the two countries in decades.

stories.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
stories.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports to last 'as long as it takes,' Defence Secretary Hegseth says
stories.summary: The US naval blockade of Iran’s ports came into effect on Monday, with President Donald Trump warning that any attack ships would be “eliminated” if they attempted to break it.

stories.title: Man wins €1m Picasso painting for €100 in charity raffle – video
stories.summary: A Parisian art enthusiast has won a Pablo Picasso painting worth more than €1m after buying a €100 raffle ticket. Ari Hodara, a 58-year-old software engineer, bought his ticket at the weekend after hearing about the raffle by chance in a restaurant. He found out he had won Head of a Woman, a portrait of Picasso’s muse Dora Maar painted in 1941 when he got a video call from Christie’s auction house in Paris. Organisers of the third '1 Picasso for €100 lottery', in aid of Alzheimer’s research, sai

stories.title: ‘How do I end a call?’: the elderly Japanese people determined to master smartphones
stories.summary: Elderly people take advantage of courses on how to navigate mobile devices and avoid ‘analogue isolation’ It’s not only young people whose gaze is fixed on tiny screens. But for these users in Tokyo, clicking and scrolling is anything but second nature. “I can’t deal with all of the apps that jump out at me,” says one. “How do I know if I’ve definitely ended a call?” asks another. Continue reading...

stories.title: Home Office ‘red flag’ error leaves German mother separated from toddler in UK
stories.summary: Liza Tobay was told settled status had been ‘red flagged’ when she tried to fly home from Germany to Scotland A German woman has been separated from her two-year-old daughter in Edinburgh after a Home Office mistake left her stranded in Dusseldorf earlier this week. stories.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
stories.summary: Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions. Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so

stories.summary: Korábban az Átlátszó arról írt, bizarr dolgokat vásárolt a cég költségén egy ottani dolgozó.

stories.title: Két napra elszökött Pitbull, máris hangnemet váltott az MTVA
stories.summary: A választás utáni két napban hirtelen hangnemet váltott a közmédia, de ez csak szerdáig tartott. A kézivezérlés visszatért, és az MTVA fő cenzora szerint marad minden a régiben.

stories.title: Egy magánklinika honlapján megjelent, hogy a vezető orvos mostantól nem lát el tiszás pácienseket
stories.summary: Azóta elérhetetlenné vált a Femcare Miniklinika weboldala.

stories.summary: A magánklinika képviselője közölte: két, távozásban lévő munkatárs hozzáférhetett a weboldal admin felületéhez.

stories.title: Megvan a Tisza Párt 140. mandátuma, a nyírbátori választókerületben is fordítottak
stories.summary: Barna-Szabó Tímea több mint ezer szavazattal legyőzte a fideszes Simon Miklóst.

stories.title: Kocsis Máté péntek este a Hír TV-be ment volna, de ennek semmi nyoma, az erről szóló tartalmakat is törölték
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezetője arról írt, hogy este 8 órakor a Péntek 8 című műsor vendége lesz, aztán törölte a posztját. A Hír TV-n a műsor eddigi aloldala is eltűnt, miután pont ma átnevezték, és áttették egy másik időpontra.

stories.title: Vidnyánszky Attila: Hogyne folytatnám
stories.summary: A Nemzeti Színház igazgatója szerint bár folyamatosan csúnya dolgokat üzengetnek neki, szeretné kitölteni a 2028-ig tartó mandátumát.

stories.title: Gönczi Gábor és Marsi Anikó maradnak a TV2-nél, csak átmeneti pihenőt tartanak
stories.summary: A 24.hu-nak azt mondták, mindenképpen vissza fognak térni, Gönczi egyéb feladataival foglalkozik is majd, csak a műsorvezetést hanyagolja.

stories.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
stories.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

stories.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
stories.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

stories.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
stories.summary: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

stories.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
stories.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

stories.title: Cyprus: Global politics felt at Candidates chess tournaments
stories.summary: The FIDE Candidates tournaments are two of the most important events in the chess calendar. This year, they took place in Cyprus. But tension relating to Russia and the war in the Middle East was never too far away.

stories.title: UK: 'Furious' PM Starmer refuses to step down over Mandelson
stories.summary: Pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer is growing over his naming of Peter Mandelson as US ambassador despite red flags. The PM says he was not told that Mandelson failed a background check before the appointment.

stories.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
stories.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

stories.title: Russia agrees to pay compensation to Azerbaijan over 2024 airliner tragedy
stories.summary: Russia and Azerbaijan have revealed that an "appropriate settlement of consequences," including compensation payment, has been reached over the Azerbaijan Airlines airliner crash in December 2024.

stories.title: Iran to blame for closing Strait of Hormuz, says Gulf leader
stories.summary: The Gulf Chief Jasem Mohamed AlBudaiwi told Euronews that Iran is responsible for weaponising the Strait to score a geopolitical win and should deal with the effects.

stories.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
stories.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

stories.title: Pakistan PM Sharif visits Doha as regional mediation gathers pace for US–Iran talks
stories.summary: With the fragile ceasefire holding and signs of de-escalation emerging, Pakistan’s mediation efforts to revive US–Iran talks are gathering pace, reflecting a broader push to contain tensions across the Middle East.

stories.title: Watch: Giant guitar-shaped forest revealed from the air in Argentina
stories.summary: Aerial footage captures the scale of “Estancia La Guitarra,” a vast tree formation in southern Córdoba province, Argentina. Created by Pedro Martin Ureta in memory of his wife, the project began in 1979 using more than 7,000 cypress and eucalyptus trees.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, Trump says in social media post
stories.summary: Trump had announced earlier on Thursday that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon would speak about halting the fighting, in what would have been the first direct conversation in more than 30 years.

stories.title: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
stories.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

stories.title: Rescuers to use air cushions in latest effort to save stranded whale
stories.summary: "Timmy" has been stranded in the Baltic Sea for weeks despite several attempts to free the ailing animal.

stories.title: School shootings a new trauma for Turkey as nation mourns
stories.summary: An expert tells the BBC the attack in Kahramanmaras was a tragedy but "not a surprise".

stories.title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
stories.summary: The BBC speaks to a student who pushed for his toe print to be taken to verify his identity.

stories.title: Runaway wolf finally captured after nine-day search in South Korea
stories.summary: The search has been marked by twists and turns, gripping South Korea and even inspiring a meme coin.

stories.title: US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon goes into effect
stories.summary: Trump extended an invitation to Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold the first direct meeting at that level in decades.

stories.title: Moment wolf on the run in South Korea is found
stories.summary: The escape of Neukgu, a two-year-old wolf, from a zoo in the city of Daejon captured national attention.

stories.title: 'Ripple effects of Iran war could lead to other conflicts,' UN development chief tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the UN Development Programme told Euronews that the impact of the Middle East conflict is being felt "way beyond the region", and that, in the absence of a resolution, the groundwork for further conflicts is being laid.

stories.title: Cameroon sees mass turnout for Pope Leo XIV visit as he calls for peace
stories.summary: Cameroon saw thousands of faithful gather on Wednesday 16 April at Bamenda’s airfield, where Pope Leo XIV celebrated an open‑air Mass and called for a “decisive change of course” towards peace.

stories.title: Netherlands Keukenhof draws global visitors for spring display
stories.summary: Netherlands opened its Keukenhof garden in Lisse for the 2026 spring season, welcoming visitors to an eight-week display featuring around seven million flowers.

stories.title: Escaped wolf Neukgu captured after nine-day search in South Korea
stories.summary: A two-year-old wolf that escaped from O-World zoo in Daejeon, South Korea, has been safely recaptured after spending nine days on the loose. The male wolf, Neukgu, was born at the zoo in 2024 and is part of an initiative to restore wolves resembling those once native to the Korean peninsula.

stories.title: China and Russia ties driving North Korean economic recovery, Seoul ministry says
stories.summary: China has long been the diplomatically isolated country's main economic backer, though Pyongyang has also drawn closer to Moscow since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

stories.title: BBC in Iran: 'Tehran does not think it has lost this war'
stories.summary: Lyse Doucet says Iranians want a solution to the long-running animosity with the US, but leaders is not willing to make a deal on Washington's terms.

stories.title: Three charged over attempted arson on Persian-language TV channel, UK police say
stories.summary: Iran International is one of several Persian‑language outlets critical of Iran's clerical leadership operating from abroad.

stories.title: Germany’s Affordable Art Fair draws crowds with artworks under €500
stories.summary: Germany is hosting the Affordable Art Fair Berlin from 16 to 19 April 2026. The event brings together dozens of galleries and focuses on artworks priced under €500, aiming to broaden access to contemporary art.

stories.title: Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees dead and missing in shipwrecks in 2025, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The Rohingya mainly leave from huge camps in Bangladesh, where more than 1 million refugees forced to flee across the border from Myanmar's Rakhine state live in squalid conditions.

stories.title: Palestinians hand over suspect in 1982 attack on Jewish restaurant in Paris
stories.summary: Hicham Harb is suspected of directing the attack in the Rue des Rosiers and acting as one of the gunmen who shot at diners.

stories.title: Italy: 25 hostages freed as Naples bank gang vanishes underground
stories.summary: A group of three masked robbers held 25 people hostage during a bank heist in Naples on Thursday 16 April 2026 before escaping through a tunnel connected to the city’s sewer network, authorities said.

stories.title: US launches fifth strike on alleged Pacific drug boat in a week, killing three
stories.summary: Wednesday’s strike brings the total of those killed in US military strikes on alleged drug boats to at least 177 Three people were killed in a US strike on another alleged drug-trafficking boat, the fifth such deadly attack in as many days, military officials have announced. US southern command said it conducted “a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations” in the eastern Pacific, without naming the alleged group, in an X post. Continue reading...

stories.title: 'Everything flew': Ukrainians describe Russian attack on apartment block – video
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine , local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour during the night with drones and missiles. In Odesa, Russian strikes killed six people, according to the head of the city’s military administration. Strikes on the capital, Kyiv, killed at least four people, including a 12-year-old, the state emergency service of Ukraine said, while another two people died in the central city of Dnipro, according to Oleksandr Ganzha, the head

stories.title: Repsol agrees Venezuela deal to boost oil production and regain control of assets
stories.summary: Spanish energy company Repsol has reached an agreement with Venezuela’s government and state-owned company Petróleos de Venezuela (PDVSA) to regain operational control of key oil assets and sharply increase production over the next three years.

stories.title: More than £1bn pledged for Sudan as humanitarian crisis deepens
stories.summary: Donors exceed funding target at Berlin conference but prospects for ceasefire remain distant More than £1bn (€1.15bn) has been pledged for war-ravaged Sudan at a conference in Berlin, eclipsing the funding target organisers had set to help mitigate the world’s largest humanitarian crisis. The financial commitments made on Wednesday will also help offset a chronic humanitarian funding shortfall in a country devastated by three years of conflict, where two-thirds of its population – 34m people – r

stories.title: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
stories.summary: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industry In Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. The meals are funded by the village’s one-megawatt solar installation, which generates roughly 10m won ($6,800) in net profit each month. “Residents eat lunch together every day, so we see each other’s faces, talk together,” say

stories.title: Pedro Pascal v Pedro Piscal: actor in legal battle with Chilean spirit brand
stories.summary: Pedro Piscal pisco is latest Chilean brand to resemble a Hollywood name – and others have fought off the lawsuits The actor Pedro Pascal is waging a legal battle against a Chilean pisco merchant who has chosen a cheeky name for his brand of the country’s national spirit: Pedro Piscal. David Herrera registered the brand name with a Chilean commercial regulator in 2023 and began selling his pisco in off-licences and restaurants. Continue reading...
